title: anagallis arvensis var. carnea
intro: Anagallis arvensis var. carnea, also known as red pimpernel, is a flowering plant in the genus Anagallis, characterized by its bright red flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Anagallis arvensis var. carnea include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Anagallis arvensis var. carnea can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
